clinicalAttribute.displayName: International Classification of Diseases for Oncology, Third Edition ICD-O-3 Histology Code
clinicalAttribute.description: The third edition of the International Classification of Diseases for Oncology, published in 2000, used principally in tumor and cancer registries for coding the site (topography) and the histology (morphology) of neoplasms._The study of the structure of the cells and their arrangement to constitute tissues and, finally, the association among these to form organs.  In pathology, the microscopic process of identifying normal and abnormal morphologic characteristics in tissues, by employing various cytochemical and immunocytochemical stains._A system of numbered categories for representation of data.

clinicalAttribute.displayName: International Classification of Diseases for Oncology, Third Edition ICD-O-3 Site Code

In pathology, the microscopic process of identifying normal and abnormal morphologic characteristics in tissues, by employing various cytochemical and immunocytochemical stains._A system of numbered categories for representation of data.

clinicalAttribute.displayName: International Classification of Diseases for Oncology, Third Edition ICD-O-3 Site Code
clinicalAttribute.description: The third edition of the International Classification of Diseases for Oncology, published in 2000, used principally in tumor and cancer registries for coding the site (topography) and the histology (morphology) of neoplasms._The description of an anatomical region or of a body part._Named locations of, or within, the body._A system of numbered categories for representation of data.

clinicalAttribute.description: The codes that represent the stage of cancer based on the nodes present (N stage) according to criteria based on multiple editions of the AJCC's Cancer Staging Manual.

clinicalAttribute.displayName: Birth from Initial Pathologic Diagnosis Date
clinicalAttribute.description: Time interval from a person's date of birth to the date of initial pathologic diagnosis, represented as a calculated number of days.

clinicalAttribute.displayName: Last Alive Less Initial Pathologic Diagnosis Date Calculated Day Value
clinicalAttribute.description: Time interval from the last day on which a person is known to be alive to the date of initial pathologic diagnosis, represented as a calculated number of days.

clinicalAttribute.displayName: International Classification of Diseases for Oncology, Third Edition ICD-O-3 Histology Code
clinicalAttribute.description: The third edition of the International Classification of Diseases for Oncology, published in 2000, used principally in tumor and cancer registries for coding the site (topography) and the histology (morphology) of neoplasms._The study of the structure of the cells and their arrangement to constitute tissues and, finally, the association among these to form organs.  In pathology, the microscopic process of identifying normal and abnormal morphologic characteristics in tissues, by employing various cytochemical and immunocytochemical stains._A system of numbered categories for representation of data.

clinicalAttribute.displayName: Birth from Initial Pathologic Diagnosis Date
clinicalAttribute.description: Time interval from a person's date of birth to the date of initial pathologic diagnosis, represented as a calculated number of days.

clinicalAttribute.displayName: Last Alive Less Initial Pathologic Diagnosis Date Calculated Day Value
clinicalAttribute.description: Time interval from the last day on which a person is known to be alive to the date of initial pathologic diagnosis, represented as a calculated number of days.

10th revision of the International Statistical Classification of Diseases and Related Health Problems.

clinicalAttribute.displayName: International Classification of Diseases for Oncology, Third Edition ICD-O-3 Histology Code
clinicalAttribute.description: The third edition of the International Classification of Diseases for Oncology, published in 2000, used principally in tumor and cancer registries for coding the site (topography) and the histology (morphology) of neoplasms._The study of the structure of the cells and their arrangement to constitute tissues and, finally, the association among these to form organs.  In pathology, the microscopic process of identifying normal and abnormal morphologic characteristics in tissues, by employing various cytochemical and immunocytochemical stains._A system of numbered categories for representation of data.

clinicalAttribute.displayName: International Classification of Diseases for Oncology, Third Edition ICD-O-3 Site Code
clinicalAttribute.description: The third edition of the International Classification of Diseases for Oncology, published in 2000, used principally in tumor and cancer registries for coding the site (topography) and the histology (morphology) of neoplasms._The description of an anatomical region or of a body part._Named locations of, or within, the body._A system of numbered categories for representation of data.

clinicalAttribute.displayName: American Joint Committee on Cancer Metastasis Stage Code
clinicalAttribute.description: Code to represent the defined absence or presence of distant spread or metastases (M) to locations via vascular channels or lymphatics beyond the regional lymph nodes, using criteria established by the American Joint Committee on Cancer (AJCC).

clinicalAttribute.displayName: Neoplasm Disease Lymph Node Stage American Joint Committee on Cancer Code
clinicalAttribute.description: The codes that represent the stage of cancer based on the nodes present (N stage) according to criteria based on multiple editions of the AJCC's Cancer Staging Manual.

clinicalAttribute.displayName: American Joint Committee on Cancer Tumor Stage Code
clinicalAttribute.description: Code of pathological T (primary tumor) to define the size or contiguous extension of the primary tumor (T), using staging criteria from the American Joint Committee on Cancer (AJCC).
